description: NPM Audit Exception Configuration - Documents accepted security vulnerabilities with mitigation strategies

xlsx.reason: No fix available on npm. Latest version 0.18.5 has known vulnerabilities. Fixes exist in 0.19.3+ but SheetJS does not publish to npm (only available via CDN). Kept as a documented runtime exception pending migration to exceljs.
mitigation: xlsx only used in read-excel.tool.ts for controlled Excel file reading
Path validation and access control checks enforce security boundaries
File size limits (100MB max) prevent DoS attacks
Only processes user-provided files with explicit permission
No arbitrary code execution paths exposed
Running in Electron main process with sandboxed renderer

vulnerabilities.title: SheetJS Regular Expression Denial of Service (ReDoS)

alternatives: exceljs - More actively maintained, better security track record (preferred migration target)
node-xlsx - Simpler library with fewer features but fewer vulnerabilities
SheetJS Pro - Commercial version with security fixes (not published to npm)
actionItems: Plan migration from xlsx to exceljs — tracked as follow-up task
No new xlsx exceptions will be added; this exception exists only because no npm fix is available

build-and-dev-tooling.reason: All advisories in this group affect dev-only transitive dependencies that are never included in the packaged Electron application. Two dependency chains are covered: (1) minimatch via eslint-plugin-sonarjs (static analysis only); (2) libxmljs2 and its tar transitive deps via @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm (SBOM generation only, dev optional). No patched versions exist for any affected package. Note: libxmljs2 is an optional peer dep — some machines may not have it installed, causing 'not found' warnings for IDs 1100657/1112329/1112659/1113300/1113375. This is expected and harmless.
mitigation: minimatch (1113466, 1113545, 1113553): ReDoS vulnerabilities pulled in only by eslint-plugin-sonarjs during lint-time static analysis. No network or untrusted input exposure in this context.

libxmljs2 (1100657): Type confusion vulnerability in an XML parsing library. Installed as a peerOptional dep of @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-library. Confirmed optional: true — the SBOM tool operates fully without it. No patched libxmljs2 release exists on npm. Never present in the production Electron bundle.
tar (1112329, 1112659, 1113300, 1113375): Path traversal and hardlink escape vulnerabilities in node-tar, pulled in transitively via libxmljs2>tar. Same dev-optional scope as libxmljs2.
Electron production bundles do not ship eslint, sonarjs, cyclonedx-npm, libxmljs2, or their transitive dependencies.
Build and CI environments are treated as trusted and isolated.

vulnerabilities.title: minimatch ReDoS via repeated wildcards with non-matching literal in pattern
minimatch ReDoS: matchOne() combinatorial backtracking via multiple non-adjacent GLOBSTAR segments
minimatch ReDoS: nested *() extglobs generate catastrophically backtracking regular expressions
libxmljs2 vulnerable to type confusion when parsing specially crafted XML
vulnerabilities.notes: dev optional — present on some machines, absent on others depending on npm install behavior for optional peers

vulnerabilities.title: Race Condition in node-tar Path Reservations via Unicode Ligature Collisions on macOS APFS
node-tar Vulnerable to Arbitrary File Creation/Overwrite via Hardlink Path Traversal
node-tar is Vulnerable to Arbitrary File Overwrite and Symlink Poisoning via Insufficient Path Sanitization
Arbitrary File Read/Write via Hardlink Target Escape Through Symlink Chain in node-tar Extraction

alternatives: Upgrade eslint-plugin-sonarjs once it ships with a patched minimatch dependency tree.
Investigate preventing libxmljs2 optional peer dep installation (e.g. npm overrides) to eliminate the critical advisory without removing @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm.
Monitor @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm releases for a version that drops or patches the libxmljs2 peer dep.
actionItems: Monitor eslint-plugin-sonarjs and @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm releases for patched dependency trees.
Re-run npm audit after any relevant tooling upgrades to verify advisories are cleared.
Revisit this exception set before expiry date (2026-09-03).

meta.changeLog: v1.4.0 (2026-03-04): Deduplicated build-and-dev-tooling IDs (removed duplicate 1113545/1113553 entries). Restored libxmljs2/tar IDs (1100657/1112329/1112659/1113300/1113375) — still present on machines where optional peer dep is installed. Added note about cross-machine variance for optional deps. v1.3.0 (2026-03-03): Added 5 IDs for libxmljs2 (critical, 1100657) and its tar transitive deps (high, 1112329/1112659/1113300/1113375) — all dev-optional via @cyclonedx/cyclonedx-npm; no patched npm release exists. v1.2.0 (2026-03-03): Removed 18 stale advisory IDs (npm rotated IDs). Removed svelte-ssr-only group (all 4 IDs stale). Pruned build-and-dev-tooling from 15 to 3 active IDs (kept 1113466, added 1113545 and 1113553 for new minimatch ReDoS advisories via eslint-plugin-sonarjs). Policy: only dev-tool transitive exceptions permitted going forward; xlsx retained as documented runtime exception pending exceljs migration.
